vulnerabilities.message: Sensitive variable logged to console. Passwords and secrets should never appear in logs. Remove this log statement or redact the sensitive field.

Detected directly writing to a Response object from user-defined input. This bypasses any HTML escaping and may expose your application to a Cross-Site-scripting (XSS) vulnerability. Instead, use 'resp.render()' to render safely escaped HTML.

User data flows into the host portion of this manually-constructed HTML. This can introduce a Cross-Site-Scripting (XSS) vulnerability if this comes from user-provided input. Consider using a sanitization library such as DOMPurify to sanitize the HTML within.

User data flows into the host portion of this manually-constructed HTML. This can introduce a Cross-Site-Scripting (XSS) vulnerability if this comes from user-provided input. Consider using a sanitization library such as DOMPurify to sanitize the HTML within.

Detected directly writing to a Response object from user-defined input. This bypasses any HTML escaping and may expose your application to a Cross-Site-scripting (XSS) vulnerability. Instead, use 'resp.render()' to render safely escaped HTML.
Hardcoded Anthropic API key assigned to a variable. Use environment variables: process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
Hardcoded credential assigned to a sensitive variable name. Use environment variables instead of hardcoding secrets in source code.
Hardcoded Supabase key assigned to a variable. Use NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY or SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY environment variables.
Hardcoded Anthropic API key assigned to a variable. Use environment variables: process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
Hardcoded credential assigned to a sensitive variable name. Use environment variables instead of hardcoding secrets in source code.
Hardcoded Supabase key assigned to a variable. Use NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY or SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY environment variables.
JWT signed/verified with a hardcoded secret. An attacker who reads your source code can forge any token. Use a long random secret from process.env.JWT_SECRET.

A hard-coded credential was detected. It is not recommended to store credentials in source-code, as this risks secrets being leaked and used by either an internal or external malicious adversary. It is recommended to use environment variables to securely provide credentials or retrieve credentials f
